narrative: We need to scale distributed manufacturing to support innovation and development in the US — ground systems infrastructure for aerospace, basic energy infrastructure, and cryogenic infrastructure for fusion and advanced energy.

items.tweet_text:
California sits at the intersection of two extremes. 

On one end is a population of extremely smart, capable Californians that have helped build the 4th largest economy in the world.

At the other end sits the same extremely smart, capable population but who have turned their https://t.co/kimr3XdHF4
items.context: Chamath Palihapitiya argues California has a bifurcated population — one side built a world-class economy, the other has weaponized talent toward regulatory and bureaucratic dysfunction.

items.caption: California has the talent and the capital. What it doesn't have is the permitting speed to build manufacturing at scale. You can't run a 4th-largest-economy on approvals that take 4 years. #ManufacturingUSA #IndustrialPolicy #Infrastructure

He is single-handedly responsible for exfiltrating me from the government and helping me realize how I could better leverage my strengths instead of remaining trapped in a concrete basement fighting bureaucracy.

He

items.caption: The talent pipeline between government and industry is one of the most underleveraged assets we have. Scaling manufacturing means getting the right operators out of basements and onto factory floors. #DefenseTech #Workforce #Manufacturing

items.tweet_text:
@Ravarora1 @GovMikeHuckabee So if a 15 year old is brainwashed for a decade to hate you and want to kill you, and trained to fight and given a gun by the terrorists, and his side has kidnapped your children, you would then refuse to fight to rescue them if it puts that armed 15 year-old in danger?

items.tweet_text:
WindWaterSolar met a peak of 144% of demand on the @CaliforniaISO grid on Saturday 2/21

Demand down 1.1% so far in '26 v '25 and down 1.7% v '23 (despite growth in EVs, heat pumps, data centers)

Fossil gas down 35% v '25 and 54% v '23.

Batteries up 39% v '25 and 304% v '23 https://t.co/aSbP7Aj99V
items.context: Mark Jacobson reports that wind, water, and solar hit 144% of demand on California's grid, with battery storage up 304% vs. 2023 and fossil gas use down sharply.
items.reception: Moderate niche engagement at 70 likes+RT — clean energy and grid infrastructure audience.
items.caption: Battery storage up 304% in two years means someone is building at scale. The question is who's making the cells, who's making the enclosures, and where. That's the manufacturing challenge nobody's solving fast enough. #EnergyStorage #Infrastructure #Manufacturing

items.tweet_text:
We’re about to witness three of the largest IPOs in history. SpaceX is targeting $1.5t. OpenAI aims for $1t. Anthropic is valued at $380b. Combined, $2.9t in market cap.

The scale is unprecedented. But the real problem isn’t the market cap. It’s the float.

Typical IPOs offer https://t.co/lTO9MvWPOk
items.context: Tomasz Tunguz analyzes the upcoming mega-IPOs of SpaceX ($1.5T), OpenAI ($1T), and Anthropic ($380B), highlighting that low float percentages could create unprecedented market dynamics.
items.reception: Surprisingly low engagement at 8 likes+RT given the substance — possibly early post or algorithmic underperformance.
items.caption: $2.9T in combined market cap and nearly all of it rides on physical infrastructure — launch pads, data centers, power plants. The capital markets are pricing in capacity we haven't built yet. #Aerospace #Infrastructure #Manufacturing

items.tweet_text:
@Allybig62 @stockscout101 Ever heard of a battery?

Or wind?

Or enhanced geothermal?

Or hydro?

Or imports?

Or heat pumps?

Or insulation to reduce energy use?

Or heat storage?
items.context: Mark Jacobson responds to a critic by listing alternatives to solar when the sun isn't shining — batteries, wind, geothermal, hydro, imports, heat pumps, insulation, and heat storage.
items.reception: Minimal engagement at 3 likes+RT — niche clean energy debate.
items.caption: Every item on that list requires manufactured hardware — batteries, turbines, heat exchangers, pumps. The energy transition isn't a software update. It's a manufacturing buildout at historic scale. #EnergyInfrastructure #Manufacturing #CleanEnergy
